授業目的: この授業の目的は、ヨーロッパの統合の歴史および欧州連合(EU)や欧州評議会のようなヨーロッパ域内の協力的枠組みと現代的課題を理解することにある。講師のThierry Rambaudは、フランスのパリ・シテ大学の公法学教授であり、公法、ヨーロッパ法を専門としている。授業は、日本の大学生が理解できるように、日本との関連も意識しながら進めていくので、興味があればぜひ受講してほしいです。
到達目標: この授業では、学生が戦後のヨーロッパ統合の歴史や、現在のEUをはじめとするヨーロッパ域内の協力枠組みを学び、ヨーロッパで生じている具体的な問題について考えられるようになることを目的としています。そしてこの学びを通じて、日本で学ぶ学生がヨーロッパに関心を広げられるようにします。
授業方法: 授業は教室での対面形式で実施する。各回の授業は、学生による15分間の発表から始まり、あわせて授業内容を紹介する資料が配布される。また、オンラインでゲストスピーカーを招いた講演およびディスカッションも予定している。Classes will be conducted face-to-face in the classroom.  Each session will begin with a 15-minute presentation by a student, and a document introducing the session will also be provided. In addition, an online lecture by a guest speaker followed by a discussion is planned.

第1回: 【Startup Week】"Face-to-face class" / 《90 minutes》Introductory session: The major stages of European integration
Reading of the relevant sections of the distributed materials.

Reading of the relevant sections of the distributed materials.

Reading of the relevant sections of the distributed materials.
第4回: 【Core Weeks③】"Face-to-face class" /《90 minutes》The Council of Europe, ‘Europe's common home’. Building a greater Europe
Reading of the relevant sections of the distributed materials.
第5回: 【Core Weeks④】"Face-to-face class" /《90 minutes》Current debates surrounding the European Court of Human Rights
Reading of the relevant sections of the distributed materials.

Reading of the relevant sections of the distributed materials.

Reading of the relevant sections of the distributed materials.

Reading of the relevant sections of the distributed materials.
第9回: 【Core Weeks⑧】"Face-to-face class" /《90 minutes》Freedom of religion in European law
Reading of the relevant sections of the distributed materials.
第10回: 【Core Weeks⑨】"Face-to-face class" /《90 minutes》Illiberal democracies in the European Union
Reading of the relevant sections of the distributed materials.
第11回: 【Core Weeks⑩】"Face-to-face class" /《90 minutes》Practical workshop – role play. Drafting a Council of Europe resolution on a current issue relating to fundamental freedoms
Reading of the relevant sections of the distributed materials.
第12回: 【Core Weeks⑪】"Face-to-face class" /《90 minutes》Japan and European Union
Reading of the relevant sections of the distributed materials.
第13回: 【Core Weeks⑫】"Face-to-face class" /《90 minutes》Written test
Reading of the relevant sections of the distributed materials.
第14回: 【Flex Hours】"Online class（Simultaneous two-way format）" /《90 minutes》Session with an external speaker as part of the course.
Reading of the relevant sections of the distributed materials.
第15回: 【Flex Hours】"Face-to-face class" /《90 minutes》New perspectives for the European project

学位授与の方針との関連Relationship to Diploma Polic: Each course is positioned within the curriculum in accordance with the diploma policy andcurriculum policy established by each undergraduate/graduate school. By proceeding with the study of this course with an awareness of the DP and CP, the following abilities expected by each undergraduate/graduate school will be developed.European Studies:1. Understanding and analytical skills regarding international issues: the ability to understand and analyze various regions of the world from different perspectives in the humanities and social sciences.2. Problem-solving skills: the ability to proactively identify problems and solve them based on appropriate methods.3. Intercultural understanding and multicultural coexistence: the ability to be sensitive to different cultures and to coexist with diverse cultures.
